acceptedQuestions.subject: Geography
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
It is a weather phenomenon characterized by a collection of thunderstorms that act together as a unified system. Rather than dissipating quickly, it can spread across a vast geographic area, such as an entire state, and persist for more than 12 hours.

Which of the following best describes the phenomenon described above?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: A Mesoscale Convective System (MCS) is a collection of thunderstorms that act as a system. It can spread across an entire state and last for more than 12 hours. The other options refer to different meteorological phenomena: supercells are typically single, highly organized storms; extratropical cyclones are large-scale low-pressure systems; and atmospheric rivers are narrow corridors of concentrated moisture.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: Mesoscale Convective Systems are large, organized thunderstorm complexes that act as a unified system and last significantly longer than isolated thunderstorms.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Mesoscale Convective System (MCS): Collection of thunderstorms that act as a system. Can spread across entire state and last >12 hours.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: A classic UPSC-style paragraph identification question. The distractors are all real meteorological terms that a candidate might confuse if they haven't studied the specific definition of an MCS.

reviewerNotes: Selected three high-quality questions that cover the diverse phenomena mentioned in the source text. Rewrote the prompts and options to align with UPSC standards, ensuring plausible distractors and clear, tight explanations.
